one_line_ask: Give the raw band press photo a bold two-tone (duotone) FX treatment, vectorize the supplied Nocturne Sessions wordmark, then run the client's authored InDesign gig-poster template through a data-merge against the event-copy CSV to export a print-ready PDF and a high-res JPEG.
brief: The Nocturne Sessions, a fictional indie synth-noir act, is releasing a new EP and needs its launch gig poster finished for print and social. The band has handed off an authored InDesign poster template (nocturne_poster_template.indd), laid out A2 portrait with a full-bleed hero-photo frame, a wordmark frame, and real InDesign data-merge fields (<<title>>, <<venue>>, <<date>>, <<support>>, <<presale>>). They also supply one raw press photo of the trio (shot on a phone in a dim rehearsal room — slightly tilted, flat/dull, cool white balance, a cluttered gear-strewn background), a flat raster promo wordmark PNG that reads "THE NOCTURNE SESSIONS," and a one-row CSV holding the show copy. The band's brief calls for "a clean modern aesthetic—nothing vintage—powered by bright, bold colours that jump off the screen and still look sharp in print," and the poster's art direction is a classic bold two-tone (duotone) gig-poster look: the hero photo rendered in just two punchy brand inks (deep indigo shadows, hot magenta highlights) rather than full colour, so it reads instantly at both A2 wall size and Instagram thumbnail size.
